Overhead power on Wattle Grove ÔÇö no easement on title for pole on Lot 14.","score":0.015151515151515152},{"document_id":"0713102e-1fda-5bb3-b953-b55265b02f14","chunk_id":"4bf36c0b-d02f-5ef0-bc42-0bcde370128f","document":"01-engagement-letter-harrison-clarke-studio.md","relative_path":"04-projects/test-project-112/02-consultant/architect/01-engagement-letter-harrison-clarke-studio.md","page_or_section":"Scope of services","snippet":"## Scope of services

Our services comprise:

1. Concept and schematic design through to DA lodgement
2. Design development and construction documentation
3. Consultant briefing, appointment support, and coordination
4. Authority and certifier liaison (BASIX, Ku-ring-gai Council, principal certifier)
5. Head builder procurement ÔÇö preparation of tender documentation, evaluation of **two to three** invited builders, and a written recommendation to you
6. Contract administration during construction to practical completion, excluding Superintendent functions under the head contract unless later varied
7. Monthly owner progress reporting and milestone advice

**Exclusions** unless separately agreed: interior design beyond joinery schedule coordination; landscape design (we will brief a landscape consultant if required); geotechnical, survey, and specialist reports (we will coordinate appointments); extra tender rounds beyond one formal head-builder tender; legal advice.","score":0.014925373134328358},{"document_id":"649a0f8f-d05d-5359-9327-ac4090584ed0","chunk_id":"26471d90-2d8f-5ed1-baaa-2f6c4a685c71","document":"05-survey-report-north-shore-surveying.md","relative_path":"04-projects/test-project-112/03-design/01-due-diligence/05-survey-report-north-shore-surveying.md","page_or_section":"Services & easements","snippet":"## Services & easements

- **Sydney Water:** 150 mm sewer in 2.0 m wide drainage easement along southern boundary. Maintenance hole at rear corner. **Build-over likely requires Sydney Water assessment.**
- **Electricity:** Overhead line to street; underground service pit in footpath ÔÇö location marked Sheet 2.
- **NBN:** Pit in footpath, north of lot.","score":0.014705882352941176},{"document_id":"7672dda4-d8b2-581f-b0e2-d2bb18c11256","chunk_id":"35268cba-8fab-555e-b4d6-b318aafb8123","document":"06-geotechnical-report-terratech.md","relative_path":"04-projects/test-project-112/03-design/01-due-diligence/06-geotechnical-report-terratech.md","page_or_section":"2. **Terratech Geotechnical Consultants Pty Ltd**
ABN 12 345 678 901 | geotech@terratech.com.au

**Project:** Chen Residence, 14 Wattle Grove Lindfield
**Client:** Harrison Clarke Studio for Michael & Sarah Chen
**Report No:** TT-GEO-2026-0442
**Investigation date:** 15 May 2026
**Issue date:** 22 May 2026

## 1. Introduction

**Geotechnical investigation report on file** for Chen Residence footing design (Terratech report TT-GEO-2026-0442).

Two machine-bored boreholes (BH-1 front, BH-2 rear) and two test pits (TP-1, TP-2) were completed to inform footing design for a proposed two-storey dwelling. Survey by North Shore Surveying indicates approx. 2.1 m site fall.

## 2. Site description

Knockdown-rebuild context. Existing single-storey brick cottage on stiffened raft (observed). Natural surface: sandy clay over residual claystone. Groundwater not encountered in boreholes at depths investigated (max 4.5 m).

## 3. Subsurface profile (summary)

| Depth (m) | Material |
| --- | --- |
| 0.0ÔÇô0.4 | Fill / topsoil |
| 0.4ÔÇô2.8 | Medium plasticity CLAY, stiff to very stiff, **reactive** |
| 2.8ÔÇô4.5+ | Weathered claystone, increasing strength |

## 4. Site classification

**AS 2870-2011 Site Classification: H1** (highly reactive clay)

Ys estimated **40ÔÇô50 mm** (normal moisture conditions). Recommend **stiffened raft slab** or **deepened beams with piers** to refusal in weathered claystone. Conventional strip footings **not recommended** without engineering redesign.

## 5. Key recommendations

1. Structural engineer to adopt H1 parameters; provide footing design to certifier
2. Site drainage critical ÔÇö maintain moisture equilibrium; no ponding against slab
3. Excavation benching may be required on cut slope at rear; temporary shoring TBC
4. Rock encountered TP-2 at 1.8 m ÔÇö allow for excavation difficulty in cost plan

## 6. Limitations

Investigation points limited to accessible areas; latent conditions (old footings, fill pockets) may differ locally. Further investigation if unusual excavation reveals undocumented fill.

**Author:** Priya Menon, MEng Geotech, Chartered","content_chars":2133,"returned_chars":2133,"content_truncated":false}

- Purpose: Geotechnical investigation to inform footing design for a proposed twoÔÇæstorey dwelling (Chen Residence, 14 Wattle Grove, Lindfield), including site classification and footing recommendations.
- Methods used:
  - Two machineÔÇæbored boreholes to depth up to about 4.5 m.
  - Two test pits.
- Outcomes documented:
  - Subsurface profile (fill/topsoil over reactive clay over weathered claystone).
  - AS 2870ÔÇæ2011 site classification (H1, highly reactive clay).
  - Footing type recommendations (stiffened raft or deepened beams with piers to weathered claystone; conventional strip footings not recommended).
  - Notes on drainage, excavation/benching, and limitations of investigation coverage.

**Borehole and test pit locations**

- **BHÔÇæ1** ÔÇô MachineÔÇæbored borehole at the **front** of the site.

- Purpose: Geotechnical investigation to inform footing design for a proposed twoÔÇæstorey dwelling (Chen Residence, 14 Wattle Grove, Lindfield), including site classification and footing recommendations.
- Methods used:
  - Two machineÔÇæbored boreholes to depth up to about 4.5 m.

**Terratech Geotechnical Consultants Pty Ltd**
ABN 12 345 678 901 | geotech@terratech.com.au

**Project:** Chen Residence, 14 Wattle Grove Lindfield
**Client:** Harrison Clarke Studio for Michael & Sarah Chen
**Report No:** TT-GEO-2026-0442
**Investigation date:** 15 May 2026
**Issue date:** 22 May 2026

## 1. Introduction

**Geotechnical investigation report on file** for Chen Residence footing design (Terratech report TT-GEO-2026-0442).

Two machine-bored boreholes (BH-1 front, BH-2 rear) and two test pits (TP-1, TP-2) were completed to inform footing design for a proposed two-storey dwelling. Survey by North Shore Surveying indicates approx. 2.1 m site fall.

## 2. Site description

Knockdown-rebuild context. Existing single-storey brick cottage on stiffened raft (observed). Natural surface: sandy clay over residual claystone. Groundwater not encountered in boreholes at depths investigated (max 4.5 m).

## 3. Subsurface profile (summary)

| Depth (m) | Material |
| --- | --- |
| 0.0ÔÇô0.4 | Fill / topsoil |
| 0.4ÔÇô2.8 | Medium plasticity CLAY, stiff to very stiff, **reactive** |
| 2.8ÔÇô4.5+ | Weathered claystone, increasing strength |

## 4. Site classification

**AS 2870-2011 Site Classification: H1** (highly reactive clay)

Ys estimated **40ÔÇô50 mm** (normal moisture conditions). Recommend **stiffened raft slab** or **deepened beams with piers** to refusal in weathered claystone. Conventional strip footings **not recommended** without engineering redesign.

## 5. Key recommendations

1. Structural engineer to adopt H1 parameters; provide footing design to certifier
2. Site drainage critical ÔÇö maintain moisture equilibrium; no ponding against slab
3. Excavation benching may be required on cut slope at rear; temporary shoring TBC
4. Rock encountered TP-2 at 1.8 m ÔÇö allow for excavation difficulty in cost plan

## 6. Limitations

Investigation points limited to accessible areas; latent conditions (old footings, fill pockets) may differ locally. Further investigation if unusual excavation reveals undocumented fill.

**Author:** Priya Menon, MEng Geotech, Chartered","content_chars":2133,"returned_chars":2133,"content_truncated":false}
